Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

Hi Alex, while I appreciate this could be useful for development/local testing, I am essentially against anything that would give to the client access to the nodemap configuration. Nodemap is server side only, and clients should never know about it.

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1
> 
> Hi Alex, while I appreciate this could be useful for development/local testing, I am essentially against anything that would give to the client access to the nodemap configuration. Nodemap is server side only, and clients should never know about it.

yes, indeed, this is not something real clients should be using.
what about limiting this option to local clients (when client share same NIDs with MDS/OST) ?

Patch Set 4:

I mean that MDS/OSS could respect this option for local clients only.

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> we do this for local recovery on MDS/OST:
> 
> static int tgt_is_local_client(const struct lu_env *env,
> 				      struct obd_export *exp)
> {
> ...
> 	if (!LNetIsPeerLocal(&req->rq_peer.nid))
> 		return 0;

Yes, as the option this patch adds would force a client to be part of the given nodemap, it would need to be strictly enforced by server side to verify it is actually a local client. We do not want this new option to introduce a way for regular clients to escape the normal nodemap arbitration.

Patch Set 4:

good, thanks

Patch Set 4: Verified+1

Build Successful

parents.subject: LU-19003 ptlrpc: prevent freed memory access in ptlrpcd_init()

commit.subject: LU-0000 nodemap: nodemap as a mount option
commit.message:
LU-0000 nodemap: nodemap as a mount option

applies only to local clients due to security reasons,
can be useful for testing purposes:

$ lctl list_nids
192.168.126.78@tcp
$ mount -t lustre ... /mnt/lustre
$ lctl get_param nodemap.default.exports
nodemap.default.exports=
[
 { nid: 0@lo, uuid: adb1d585-d975-48c6-87e3-faacf64fdedf },
 { nid: 0@lo, uuid: lustre-MDT0000-mdtlov_UUID },
 { nid: 0@lo, uuid: lustre-MDT0000-lwp-MDT0000_UUID },
 { nid: 0@lo, uuid: lustre-MDT0000-lwp-OST0000_UUID }
]
$ lctl nodemap_add testnm
$ mount -t lustre -o nodemap=testnm ... /mnt/lustre2
$ lctl get_param nodemap.testnm.exports
